clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Provide evidence-based recommendations for managing renal artery stenosis in hypertensive adults
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Initial diagnosis of renal artery stenosis, treatment planning, medication failure assessment, revascularization decision-making
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Medical therapy recommendation OR revascularization referral based on disease type and treatment response

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for renal artery stenosis assessment
Assess hypertension status and kidney function
Evaluate medical management failure (resistant HTN, worsening kidney function, acute HF)
Display COR and LOE based on clinical scenario

validationRules: If atherosclerotic and medical management failed, capture specific failure types

rules: IF stenosisType = 'atherosclerotic' AND patient has hypertension → Class 1, LOE A: Recommend medical therapy to reduce kidney and CVD morbidity/mortality

IF stenosisType = 'atherosclerotic' AND medicalManagementFailed = true (resistant HTN, worsening kidney function, OR acute HF) → Class 2a, LOE C-EO: Reasonable to refer for revascularization (percutaneous angioplasty/stent)
IF stenosisType = 'nonatherosclerotic' (fibromuscular dysplasia) → Class 2b, LOE C-LD: May be reasonable to refer for revascularization (percutaneous angioplasty only, no stent)
Apply COR and LOE independently per ACC/AHA framework

categories.name: Class 2a
categories.condition: Atherosclerotic RAS + medical management failed (resistant HTN, worsening kidney function, OR acute HF)

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: For Class 1: Initiate medical therapy; For Class 2a/2b: Refer to interventional cardiology or vascular surgery
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Monitor for medical management failure (resistant HTN, kidney function decline, HF symptoms)
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Reassess if medical management fails or new symptoms develop
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag Class 2a criteria for expedited referral when medical management fails
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log recommendation, COR/LOE, and referral status to health record
